title: RepoRoutingPolicy
description: Declarative routing rules for cubie-* and other Centillion repos. Consumed by routing/auto_route.py. Every file in a consumer repo must satisfy exactly one applicable rule (or none, meaning no constraint). CI gate fails the PR if any file violates an applicable rule.

version.description: Policy schema version. Monotonic. Breaking changes increment this.

description.description: Free-form description of THIS policy (per consumer). Optional.

id.description: Kebab-case unique rule id. Cited in commit messages: 'Routing: rule=<id>'.

reason.description: WHY this rule exists. Required so future maintainers understand intent.

pattern.description: Glob (with {} alternation and [] character classes) matching paths that THIS rule governs. Mutually exclusive with forbid_pattern.

forbid_pattern.description: Glob matching paths that MUST NOT exist. Mutually exclusive with pattern.

location.description: Where the matched file currently must live to trigger this rule. '/' = repo root; 'any' = any location; otherwise a path. Default 'any'.

exceptions.description: Glob patterns to SKIP even if pattern matches. Useful for excluding already-canonical files.

keep.description: Basenames to KEEP at location even though they match pattern. Paired with move_rest_to.

move_rest_to.description: Destination for files matching pattern but NOT in keep[].

extract_to.description: Path to a JSONL/CSV file where this rule appends item entries (for content extraction rules). Engine emits a TODO log, does not implement content parsing in v1.

then_archive_to.description: After extract_to, source file moves here.

keep_basename.description: Preserve the original filename at the destination. If false, target must use {lower-kebab-name}.

condition.description: Optional condition DSL expression. Supported predicates: all_checkboxes_checked, any_checkbox_unchecked, age > Nd, has_keyword(<str>), matches_regex(<re>), referenced_by_count > N. Combine with 'and'/'or'.

auto_apply.description: If true, weekly cron CI workflow applies this rule automatically.
